{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『古事記注解』|date=1993年6月|publisher=笠間書院|author=Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori|isbn=978-4-305-60010-3|volume=2}}

In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.

{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.

== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). {{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.

== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | generation = {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} 2nd Generation | predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} | successor = {{ill|Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|宇比地邇神|lt=Ubi-jini-no-Mikoto}} & {{ill|Subi-jini-no-Mikoto|ja|須比智邇神|lt=Subi-jini-no-Mikoto}} | kami_kind = {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天津神|lt=Tenjin}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokabushino-Mikoto|ja|豊香節野尊|lt=Toyokabushino-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto|ja|浮経野豊買尊|lt=Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyokuni-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国野尊|lt=Toyokuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} etc. }}}} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神道|lt=Shinto}} deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]].
== Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui}}.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}} and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}} of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}.
The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref>
In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref>
== Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai|ja|神世七代|lt=Jinsei Shichidai}}, succeeding {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, and is a {{ill|Dokugami|ja|独神|lt=Dokugami}}, who immediately concealed himself.
In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto}}, who is noted as a male deity.
In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear.
Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths.
== Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|lt=Inebe Shrine}} (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Uchikubo) {{Kamiyonanayo}}
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『古事記注解』|date=1993rd year 6月|publisher=笠間書院|author=Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori|isbn=978-4-305-60010-3|volume=2}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 豊雲野神 | 世代名 = [[神世七代]] 第二代 | 先代= [[国之常立神]] | 次代= [[宇比地邇神]]・[[須比智邇神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[天津神]] | 全名 = 豊雲野神 | 別名 = 豊斟渟尊、豊国主尊、豊組野尊、豊香節野尊、浮経野豊買尊、豊国野尊、豊齧野尊、葉木国野尊、国見野尊、見野尊 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[熊野速玉大社]] * [[物部神社]] 等 }} |画像=Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg|画像説明=（『神仏図会』）}} '''トヨクモノ'''（豊雲野）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''豊雲野神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''豊斟渟尊'''（トヨクムヌ）と表記される。
『古事記』において[[神世七代]]の第二代の神とされ、『日本書紀』では[[天地開闢 (日本神話)|天地開闢]]に登場する神世七代の第三代の神とされる。また神世七代の最後の[[独神]]である。
「豊かな（＝トヨ）雲（＝クモ、ノ）」の意であり、雲を神格化した存在とされる<ref>神野志隆光・山口佳紀『古事記注解』２</ref>。
『古事記』原文にはアクセントの注記があり、「雲」は音を上げて発音するため、神名は「豊雲、野」ではない。従って名義は「豊かな野で、雲の覆う野」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 ==記述== 『古事記』では、神代七代の二番目、[[国之常立神]]の次に化生したとしている。国之常立神と同じく[[独神]]であり、すぐに身を隠したとある。
『日本書紀』本文では、天地開闢の後、国常立尊、[[国狭槌尊]]の次の三番目に豊斟渟尊が化生したとしており、これらの三柱の神は男神であると記している。
第一の一書では、国常立尊・国狭槌尊の次の三番目に化生した神を'''豊国主尊'''（とよくにぬしのみこと）とし、別名がして'''豊組野尊'''（とよくむののみこと）、'''豊香節野尊'''（とよかぶののみこと）、'''浮経野豊買尊'''（うかぶののとよかふのみこと）、'''豊国野尊'''（とよくにののみこと）、'''豊齧野尊'''（とよかぶののみこと）、'''葉木国野尊'''（はこくにののみこと）、'''見野尊'''（みののみこと）であると記している。「豊」がつく名前が多く、豊雲野神・豊斟渟尊と同一神格と考えられている。第二から第六の一書には、同一神とみられる神名は登場しない。
『古事記』・『日本書紀』とも、これ以降、豊雲野神が神話に登場することはない。 == 祀る神社 == * [[物部神社]] 境内 神代七代社（[[島根県]][[大田市]]川合町） * [[忌部神社]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]東忌部町） * [[穂見諏訪十五所神社]]（[[山梨県]][[北杜市]][[長坂町]]） * [[熊野速玉大社]]（[[和歌山県]][[新宮市]]新宮） * [[宮浦宮]]（[[鹿児島県]][[霧島市]][[福山町]]） * 埜神社（[[愛知県]][[豊田市]][[野口町 (豊田市)|野口町]]） * [[大縣神社]] 境内 大国恵比須神社（愛知県[[犬山市]]宮山） * [[荒橿神社]]（[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[茂木町]]） * [[二荒山神社]] 境内 十二社（栃木県[[宇都宮市]]馬場通り） * [[胸形神社]]（栃木県[[鹿沼市]][[村井町]]） * 三明神社（愛知県[[犬山市]]字内久保） ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <references/> ==参考文献== * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記注解 |date=1993年6月 |publisher=[[笠間書院]] |author=[[神野志隆光]]、[[山口佳紀]] 編著 |isbn=978-4-305-60010-3 |pages= |volume=２}} == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/toyokumononokami/ 豊雲野神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよくもの}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:独神]] [[Category:創造神]] [[Category:大地神]]

天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, {{ill|Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊国主尊|lt=Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊組野尊|lt=Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyogumi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|ja|豊齧野尊|lt=Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|ja|葉木国野尊|lt=Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagikuni-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|ja|国見野尊|lt=Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikonomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minono-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|qq=|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|qq=|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Hayatama Taisha]] * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munagata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City,
