shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|1=Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōagata Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakashi Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niarayama Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munagata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Toyokumono-no-Mikoto | alias = {{ill|Minono-Mikoto|ja|見野尊|lt=Minono-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minono-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrine = {{Plainlist| * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōagata Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakashi Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niarayama Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munagata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|1=Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munagata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|1=Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munagata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|1=Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōagata Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja|大国恵比須神社|lt=Okuniemeishu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuniemeishu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, Miyama) * {{ill|Arakashi Shrine|ja|荒橿神社|lt=Arakashi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakashi Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Haga District, Mogi Town) * {{ill|Niarayama Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|lt=Niarayama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niarayama Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata Shrine|ja|胸形神社|lt=Munagata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munagata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Matsue City, Higashiinebe Town) * {{ill|Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|lt=Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homi Suwa Fifteen Shrines|1=Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106838012}} (Yamanashi Prefecture, Hokuto City, Nagasaka Town) * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Wakayama Prefecture, Shingu City, Shingu) * {{ill|Miyaura Shrine|ja|宮浦宮|lt=Miyaura Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaura Shrine|qq=}} (Kagoshima Prefecture, Kirishima City, Fukumamachi) * {{ill|Yashiro Shrine|ja|埜神社|lt=Yashiro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Toyota City, Noguchi Town) * {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|ja|大縣神社|lt=Okitsu Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}} – within the precincts, twelve shrines (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City, Baba Street) * {{ill|Munagata * {{ill|Monobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} etc. }}| image= File:Toyokuninu shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} – within the precincts, {{ill|Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja|神代七代社|lt=Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Shichidai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Ōta City, Kawai Town) * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inebe Shrine]] (Shimane Prefecture, Kanuma City, Murai Town) * {{ill|Sanmyo Shrine|ja|三明神社|lt=Sanmyo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Aichi Prefecture, Inuyama City, shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} '''Toyokumono-no-Mikoto''' (豊雲野神, Toyokumono-no-Mikoto) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]. == Overview == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is known as '''Toyokumono-no-Kami''' (豊雲野神), and in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is referred to as '''Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto''' (豊斟渟尊). He is also venerated as an important deity in {{ill|Hachigoden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|lt=Hachigoden|lt=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachigoden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}. In the '''Kojiki Den''' (『古事記伝』), there is a theory that equates him with {{ill|Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|ja|ツヌグイ・イクグイ|lt=Ikugui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui_and_Ikugui|qq=}}. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), he is regarded as the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]] and the third deity emerging from the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku). He is also considered the last [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]] of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]]. The name "Toyokumo-no-Kami" (豊雲野神) means "abundant clouds," indicating that he is a deified cloud entity.<ref>Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori, 『古事記注解』, Volume 2.</ref> In the original text of the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), there are accent notes indicating that "cloud" (雲) is pronounced with a rising intonation, implying that the name is not "Toyokumo, No" but rather "Toyokumo-no-Kami," meaning "abundant clouds over the field."<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, 『古事記』.</ref> == Description == In the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), he is the second deity of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Jinsei Shichidai]], succeeding [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto]], and is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Dokugami]], who immediately concealed himself. In the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』), after the creation of the heavens and earth (天地開闢, Tenchi Kaiyaku), {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|lt=Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}, who is noted as a male deity. In the first volume, the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) identifies the third deity after {{ill|Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as '''Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto''' (豊国主尊), also known by aliases '''Toyogumi-no-Mikoto''' (豊組野尊), '''Toyokabushino-Mikoto''' (豊香節野尊), '''Ukakeyuyami-no-Mikoto''' (浮経野豊買尊), '''Toyokuni-no-Mikoto''' (豊国野尊), '''Toyoneguchi-no-Mikoto''' (豊齧野尊), '''Hagikuni-no-Mikoto''' (葉木国野尊), '''Mikonomi-no-Mikoto''' (国見野尊), and '''Minono-Mikoto''' (見野尊). The prevalence of names starting with "豊" (豊, "toyoo" meaning "abundant") suggests that Toyokumono-no-Kami and Toyoshinrei-no-Mikoto are manifestations of the same deity. In volumes two through six, names believed to refer to the same deity do not appear. Neither the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』) nor the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』) mention Toyokumono-no-Mikoto in later myths. == Shrines Enshrining Toyokumono-no-Mikoto == * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe
